attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""A/B CERTIFIER for the PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS map-then-reduce port (this worktree).
2	
3	Proves the parallel path (_compose_section_per_basket with workers=16) produces the BYTE-IDENTICAL
4	ordered output list as the serial path (workers=1) on the SAME per-basket producer outputs — i.e. the
5	reconciled MAP/REDUCE dedup is verdict-set-identical to this worktree's serial loop, and has no
6	shared-state race under real thread interleaving.
7	
8	Method: monkeypatch the per-basket producers the MAP calls with DETERMINISTIC-per-basket functions
9	(same basket -> same text in both arms) that inject a RANDOM sleep (forces the ThreadPool to interleave
10	in a different order each call). The producer outputs are crafted to hit every REDUCE branch:
11	  - unique units (kept)
12	  - exact duplicate text+span of an earlier basket (subset+text dedup -> dropped)
13	  - same footprint, NO new number (footprint-number collapse -> dropped)
14	  - same footprint, NEW number (survives)
15	  - internal §3.5 insufficient-evidence marker (whole basket skipped, aux discarded)
16	  - aux units (companion/qualifier/distinct) incl. an aux that duplicates a main (aux dedup)
17	If serial != parallel on ANY of 200 randomized trials, the port has a race. Zero LLM.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import os
22	import random
23	import sys
24	import time
25	
26	os.environ["PG_COMPANION_FIGURE_COMPOSE"] = "1"
27	os.environ["PG_QUALIFIER_ELABORATION"] = "1"
28	os.environ["PG_SUBTOPIC_ADDITIVE_FACTS"] = "1"
29	
30	import src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose as vc  # noqa: E402
31	
32	MARKER = "[insufficient verified evidence for this basket]"
33	
34	
35	def _tok(ev: str, s: int, e: int) -> str:
36	    return f"[#ev:{ev}:{s}-{e}]"
37	
38	
39	# Deterministic per-basket MAIN text. Baskets are ints 0..N-1.
40	def _main_text(b: int) -> str:
41	    if b == 3:
42	        return MARKER  # §3.5: whole basket (incl aux) must be skipped
43	    if b == 5:
44	        # exact duplicate of basket 0's text+span -> subset+text dedup drop
45	        return f"Finding zero {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
46	    if b == 8:
47	        # same footprint as basket 0 (ev0:0-10) but NO new number token beyond '5%' -> collapse
48	        return f"Restated finding {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
49	    if b == 9:
50	        # same footprint as basket 0 but a NEW number (12%) -> survives
51	        return f"Extended finding {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rose to 12%."
52	    if b == 0:
53	        return f"Finding zero {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
54	    return f"Finding {b} {_tok(f'ev{b}', b, b + 20)} value {b}0%."
55	
56	
57	def _aux_texts(b: int) -> list[str]:
58	    outs = []
59	    if b in (2, 6):
60	        outs.append(f"Companion {b} {_tok(f'evc{b}', 0, 15)} extra {b}1%.")
61	    if b == 6:
62	        # aux that duplicates basket 6's own companion -> aux dedup drop
63	        outs.append(f"Companion 6 {_tok('evc6', 0, 15)} extra 61%.")
64	    if b in (4, 7):
65	        outs.append(f"Qualifier {b} {_tok(f'evq{b}', 5, 40)} scope {b}.")
66	    return outs
67	
68	
69	def _sleep():
70	    time.sleep(random.uniform(0, 0.004))
71	
72	
73	def _install_stubs():
74	    vc._distinct_origin_supports = lambda basket: []  # force single-basket path (no multicited)
75	
76	    def _stub_compose_one_basket(basket, evidence_pool, **kw):
77	        _sleep()
78	        return _main_text(basket)
79	
80	    vc._compose_one_basket = _stub_compose_one_basket
81	
82	    def _stub_companion(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
83	        _sleep()
84	        return [t for t in _aux_texts(basket) if t.startswith("Companion")]
85	
86	    def _stub_qualifier(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
87	        _sleep()
88	        return [t for t in _aux_texts(basket) if t.startswith("Qualifier")]
89	
90	    def _stub_distinct(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
91	        _sleep()
92	        return []
93	
94	    vc.compose_companion_figure_units = _stub_companion
95	    vc.compose_qualifier_elaboration_units = _stub_qualifier
96	    vc.compose_distinct_fact_units = _stub_distinct
97	
98	
99	def _run(workers: int, baskets: list[int]) -> list[str]:
100	    os.environ["PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS"] = str(workers)
101	    return vc._compose_section_per_basket(
102	        baskets, {}, writer_fn=lambda b, p: "", verify_fn=lambda *a, **k: None,
103	    )
104	
105	
106	def main() -> int:
107	    _install_stubs()
108	    baskets = list(range(40))
109	    serial = _run(1, baskets)
110	    # sanity: the serial path exercised real dedup (dropped at least the crafted duplicates)
111	    print(f"serial units kept = {len(serial)} (of {len(baskets)} baskets + aux)")
112	    assert MARKER not in "\n".join(serial), "§3.5 marker leaked"
113	    assert not any("Finding 3 " in u for u in serial), "basket 3 (marker) should be fully skipped"
114	
115	    trials = 200
116	    fails = 0
117	    for i in range(trials):
118	        random.seed(1000 + i)
119	        par = _run(16, baskets)
120	        if par != serial:
121	            fails += 1
122	            if fails <= 3:
123	                print(f"  DIVERGENCE trial {i}: serial={len(serial)} par={len(par)}")
124	                for a, b in zip(serial, par):
125	                    if a != b:
126	                        print(f"    serial: {a!r}\n    par   : {b!r}")
127	                        break
128	    if fails:
129	        print(f"FAIL: {fails}/{trials} parallel trials DIVERGED from serial")
130	        return 1
131	    print(f"PASS: {trials}/{trials} parallel (workers=16) trials BYTE-IDENTICAL to serial (workers=1)")
132	    print("VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: IDENTICAL under randomized thread interleaving")
133	    return 0
134	
135	
136	if __name__ == "__main__":
137	    sys.exit(main())
138

1	"""
2	Compose-stage validation using OpenAI as substitute for OpenRouter (which is 402 blocked).
3	
4	PURPOSE: validate the LLM compose path of `compose_from_wiki()` end-to-end.
5	This is the ONE pipeline stage we have not exercised on real LLM output.
6	
7	WHAT THIS PROVES:
8	- The wiki structure feeds correctly into the composer
9	- The LLM honors [REF:N] citations with the 5-lens scaffold
10	- _scrub_cot() runs cleanly on real model output
11	- [REF:N] -> [N] resolution works on real prose
12	- Final report assembles with bibliography
13	- Quality gate evaluates correctly
14	
15	WHAT THIS DOES NOT PROVE:
16	- Final G-Eval score (model is gpt-4o-mini, not Qwen 3.5 Plus)
17	- Real LLM evidence extraction quality from raw markdown
18	- Production timeout behavior at 600-evidence scale
19	
20	This is a mechanical-pipeline validator, not a quality benchmark.
21	"""
22	import asyncio
23	import os
24	import re
25	import sys
26	import time
27	from dataclasses import dataclass
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any, Optional
30	
31	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
32	
33	from dotenv import load_dotenv
34	load_dotenv()
35	
36	os.environ["PG_WIKI_ENABLED"] = "1"
37	os.environ["PG_WIKI_5LENS"] = "1"
38	
39	import logging
40	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
41	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
42	
43	
44	# ── OpenAI shim that mimics OpenRouterClient.generate() ──────────────
45	
46	@dataclass
47	class _ShimResponse:
48	    content: str
49	    reasoning: Optional[str] = None
50	    input_tokens: int = 0
51	    output_tokens: int = 0
52	    reasoning_tokens: int = 0
53	    model: str = "gpt-4o-mini"
54	    duration_ms: float = 0
55	    raw_response: Optional[dict] = None
56	
57	
58	class OpenAIShimClient:
59	    """Minimal OpenAI client with the OpenRouterClient.generate() interface.
60	
61	    Defaults to gpt-5 (the strongest available reasoning model). gpt-5 quirks:
62	    - Uses `max_completion_tokens`, NOT `max_tokens`
63	    - Only supports default temperature=1.0 (rejects custom temp)
64	    - Burns reasoning tokens BEFORE producing content — needs generous budget
65	    """
66	
67	    # Models that share gpt-5's reasoning quirks (flagged for param handling)
68	    _REASONING_MODELS = {"gpt-5", "o3", "o3-mini", "o1", "o1-preview", "o1-mini"}
69	
70	    def __init__(self, model: str = "gpt-5"):
71	        import httpx
72	        self.model = model
73	        self.is_reasoning = any(model.startswith(m) for m in self._REASONING_MODELS)
74	        self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(
75	            base_url="https://api.openai.com/v1",
76	            headers={
77	                "Authorization": f"Bearer {os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY', '')}",
78	                "Content-Type": "application/json",
79	            },
80	            timeout=httpx.Timeout(300.0, connect=30.0),
81	        )
82	        self.calls = 0
83	        self.total_input = 0
84	        self.total_output = 0
85	        self.total_reasoning = 0
86	
87	    async def close(self):
88	        await self._client.aclose()
89	
90	    async def generate(
91	        self,
92	        prompt: str,
93	        system: str = "",
94	        max_tokens: int = 4096,
95	        temperature: float = 0.3,
96	        timeout: Optional[float] = None,
97	        **kwargs,
98	    ) -> _ShimResponse:
99	        messages = []
100	        if system:
101	            messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system})
102	        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
103	
104	        body: dict[str, Any] = {
105	            "model": self.model,
106	            "messages": messages,
107	        }
108	
109	        if self.is_reasoning:
110	            # gpt-5/o3 burn reasoning tokens before content. Triple the budget
111	            # so reasoning doesn't starve the prose output.
112	            body["max_completion_tokens"] = max(max_tokens * 3, 8000)
113	            # No temperature — gpt-5 only accepts default 1.0
114	        else:
115	            body["max_tokens"] = max_tokens
116	            body["temperature"] = temperature
117	
118	        start = time.monotonic()
119	        resp = await self._client.post("/chat/completions", json=body)
120	        resp.raise_for_status()
121	        data = resp.json()
122	        duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000
123	
124	        content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] or ""
125	        usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
126	        in_tok = usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
127	        out_tok = usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
128	        rsn_tok = (usage.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}).get("reasoning_tokens", 0)
129	
130	        self.calls += 1
131	        self.total_input += in_tok
132	        self.total_output += out_tok
133	        self.total_reasoning += rsn_tok
134	
135	        return _ShimResponse(
136	            content=content,
137	            input_tokens=in_tok,
138	            output_tokens=out_tok,
139	            reasoning_tokens=rsn_tok,
140	            model=self.model,
141	            duration_ms=duration_ms,
142	        )
143

217	            "IF Effects on Blood Pressure Review",

223	            "TRE Effect on Apolipoprotein B (AJCN 2023)",
224	            "TRE reduced apoB by 7.4 mg/dL (95% CI -12.8 to -2.0) and improved insulin sensitivity by 24%.",

233	        ),

236	            "IF and Eating Disorder Risk (Nature Med 2022)",

238	            "In a cohort of 2762 young adults, intermittent fasting was associated with higher binge-eating disorder risk (adjusted OR 1.49, 95% CI 1.21-1.84).",

255	def _outline() -> list[dict]:
256	    return [
257	        {"section_id": "s01", "title": "Weight Loss and Body Composition",
258	         "description": "Effect of intermittent fasting protocols on body weight, fat mass, and adherence."},
259	        {"section_id": "s02", "title": "Cardiovascular and Lipid Health",

267	
268	async def main():
269	    if not os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
270	        print("ERROR: OPENAI_API_KEY not set in .env")
271	        return 1
272	
273	    model_name = os.getenv("OPENAI_TEST_MODEL", "gpt-5")
274	    print("=" * 70)
275	    print(f"WIKI COMPOSE VALIDATION via OpenAI shim ({model_name})")
276	    print("=" * 70)
277	
278	    # ── Stage 1: Build wiki from seed evidence ───────────────────────
279	    print("\n[1/4] Building wiki from 13 seed evidence pieces")
280	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_builder import build_wiki
281	
282	    evidence = _seed_evidence()
283	    outline = _outline()
284	    print(f"  Evidence: {len(evidence)} pieces")
285	    print(f"  Outline:  {len(outline)} sections")
286	
287	    wiki = build_wiki(
288	        evidence=evidence, outline=outline,
289	        query="intermittent fasting health benefits and risks",
290	        vector_id="OPENAI_COMPOSE_VALIDATION",
291	    )
292	
293	    total_claims = sum(len(c) for c in wiki.section_claims.values())
294	    print(f"  Built wiki: {total_claims} claims, {len(wiki.bibliography)} bib entries")
295	    for sid, claims in wiki.section_claims.items():
296	        sec_title = next((s["title"] for s in outline if s["section_id"] == sid), sid)
297	        print(f"    {sid}: {len(claims):2d} claims | {sec_title[:45]}")
298	
299	    if total_claims == 0:
300	        print("\nFAIL: wiki has no claims — cannot proceed")
301	        return 1
302	
303	    # ── Stage 2: Compose with OpenAI shim ────────────────────────────
304	    print(f"\n[2/4] Composing report via OpenAI shim ({model_name})")
305	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_composer import compose_from_wiki
306	
307	    client = OpenAIShimClient(model=model_name)
308	    start = time.monotonic()
309	    try:
310	        result = await compose_from_wiki(
311	            client=client,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
312	            wiki_result=wiki,
313	            query="intermittent fasting health benefits and risks",
314	            outline=outline,
315	        )
316	    except Exception as exc:
317	        print(f"\nFAIL: compose_from_wiki raised: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
318	        await client.close()
319	        return 1
320	
321	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
322	    await client.close()
323	
324	    # ── Stage 3: Inspect output ─────────────────────────────────────
325	    print(f"\n[3/4] Compose complete in {elapsed:.0f}s ({client.calls} LLM calls)")
326	    print(f"  Status: {result['status']}")
327	    print(f"  Quality gate: {result['quality_gate_result']}")
328	
329	    sections = result["sections"]
330	    qm = result["quality_metrics"]
331	    print(f"\n  Sections composed: {len(sections)}/{len(outline)}")
332	    print(f"  Total words:       {qm['total_words']}")
333	    print(f"  Total citations:   {qm['total_citations']}")
334	    print(f"  Unique sources:    {qm['unique_sources']}")
335	    print(f"  Zero-cite sec:     {qm['zero_cite_sections']}")
336	    print(f"  Avg cite/sec:      {qm['avg_citations_per_section']:.1f}")
337	
338	    for s in sections:
339	        print(f"    {s['section_id']}: {s['word_count']:4d} words, "
340	              f"{len(s['citation_ids']):2d} unique cites — {s['title'][:40]}")
341	
342	    # ── Stage 4: Validation checks ──────────────────────────────────
343	    print(f"\n[4/4] Validation checks")
344	    checks = []
345	
346	    # V1: All sections produced content
347	    v1 = len(sections) == len(outline)
348	    checks.append(("V1 all sections composed",
349	                   v1, f"{len(sections)}/{len(outline)}"))
350	
351	    # V2: No CoT leakage (look for telltale phrases)
352	    cot_phrases = ["let me", "i need to", "first, i'll", "thinking about",
353	                   "let's start", "i should", "i will write", "okay,",
354	                   "as an ai", "i'll write", "here's a"]
355	    cot_hits = []
356	    for s in sections:
357	        low = s["content"].lower()
358	        for ph in cot_phrases:
359	            if ph in low:
360	                cot_hits.append((s["section_id"], ph))
361	    v2 = len(cot_hits) == 0
362	    checks.append(("V2 no CoT leakage", v2,
363	                   f"{len(cot_hits)} hits" if cot_hits else "clean"))
364	
365	    # V3: All citation prefixes resolved → bare [N].
366	    # The model intermittently emits [REF:N], [CITE:N], or [Ref:N].
367	    leftover_pattern = re.compile(r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):\d+\]")
368	    leftover_refs = []
369	    for s in sections:
370	        if leftover_pattern.search(s["content"]):
371	            leftover_refs.append(s["section_id"])
372	    # Also check the abstract via final_report
373	    abstract_leak = bool(leftover_pattern.search(result.get("final_report", "")))
374	    if abstract_leak:
375	        leftover_refs.append("abstract")
376	    v3 = len(leftover_refs) == 0
377	    checks.append(("V3 citation prefixes resolved",
378	                   v3, f"{len(leftover_refs)} leaks" if leftover_refs else "clean"))
379	
380	    # V3b: No literal [N] placeholder (model writing template literal as citation)
381	    placeholder_hits = []
382	    if re.search(r"\[N\]", result.get("final_report", "")):
383	        placeholder_hits.append("final_report")
384	    for s in sections:
385	        if re.search(r"\[N\]", s["content"]):
386	            placeholder_hits.append(s["section_id"])
387	    v3b = len(placeholder_hits) == 0
388	    checks.append(("V3b no literal [N] placeholder",
389	                   v3b, f"{len(placeholder_hits)} leaks" if placeholder_hits else "clean"))
390	
391	    # V4: Citation density relative to the available source pool.
392	    # Absolute ceiling = (unique_sources / total_words) * 100. With only 13
393	    # seed sources and ~2000 words, the ceiling is ~0.65/100w. We require
394	    # the model to hit at least 80% of that ceiling, i.e. it must use most
395	    # of the available sources rather than recycling a few. At production
396	    # scale (200 sources / 10K words) this becomes 0.8 * 2.0 = 1.6/100w.
397	    avg_density = (qm["total_citations"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
398	    ceiling = (qm["unique_sources"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
399	    target_density = ceiling * 0.8
400	    v4 = avg_density >= target_density
401	    checks.append(("V4 citation density vs pool",
402	                   v4, f"{avg_density:.2f}/100w vs target {target_density:.2f} "
